__inputs.name: DS_PROMETHEUS
__inputs.label: Prometheus
__inputs.description: Grafana Cloud Mimir/Prometheus data source receiving OTLP metrics via the collector (see otel-collector-config.yaml). Metric names below are the OTLP→Prometheus-translated forms of the five instruments in app/telemetry/metrics.py: a monotonic Sum with unit "1" becomes `<name>_total`; a Histogram with unit "ms" becomes `<name>_milliseconds_{bucket,sum,count}`. All five instruments are Cumulative sums that never reset within a demo session (verified live via `docker compose logs collector`), so the cost/count panels (1, 2, 3, 4, 6) read the raw counter directly — `sum by (...) (metric_total)` — rather than wrapping it in `increase()`/`rate()`. This is a deliberate departure from the earlier rev-2 fix: `make demo` seeds exactly ONE completed run per agent, i.e. a single-increment counter, and `increase(counter[$__range])` extrapolates over so few samples that it can round below 1 and format as 0 (confirmed: raw `runs_completed_total{agent_id="agent-simple",status="completed"}` is a clean integer `1`, but `increase()` over the same window was observed reading as 0 in the legend). Reading the raw cumulative value has no extrapolation math, so `lastNotNull` always reflects the true accumulated total. Only the duration histogram (panel 5) still needs `increase()` feeding `histogram_quantile` — that's the only mathematically valid way to compute a quantile from bucket counts, low-volume or not. If this dashboard is ever pointed at continuous production traffic instead of one-off demo bursts, panels 1/2/3/4/6 should switch back to `rate()`/`increase()` over a bounded window so old activity ages out.

panels.title: Cost over time by agent
panels.description: Implied action: which agent is driving spend right now — the first thing to look at when a bill spikes. Plots the raw cumulative counter (cost accrued since process start) rather than a windowed increase() — see the datasource note on why. Legend shows the actual accrued total per agent.

panels.title: Output-token share by agent
panels.description: Implied action: ratio drift is the signal — an agent whose output share creeps up is generating more per call, at output-token prices (typically 3–5x input). Ratio of raw cumulative counters — no increase() extrapolation to distort a low-sample-count ratio.

panels.title: Run outcome rate by agent
panels.description: Implied action: failed runs still cost money — a rising failed/cancelled count for one agent, relative to its completed count, is a reliability problem independent of the cost panel. Legend shows the raw cumulative count per agent/status — reading the counter directly instead of increase() avoids extrapolation rounding a single-run demo count down to 0.

panels.title: Step failure/retry rate by step type
panels.description: Implied action: localizes problems to a specific tool or model call rather than "the agent is flaky" — tells you which step type to go fix. Legend shows the raw cumulative count per step_type/outcome — same raw-counter fix as the outcome-rate panel.

panels.title: Run duration p50 / p95
panels.description: Implied action: p95 outliers with exemplar click-through (M8.T2) jump straight to the actual slow trace — click a diamond marker on the p95 series. Uses increase() rather than rate() feeding histogram_quantile, which is the standard approach for low-volume/bursty series (Prometheus's own guidance) and what keeps this panel populated for a handful of demo runs instead of empty.

panels.title: Cost per completed run
panels.description: Implied action: unit economics — is this agent getting cheaper or more expensive per successful outcome over time, independent of volume. Ratio of raw cumulative counters, same rationale as the other count/cost panels.
